records.ety: [L. stagnans, -antis, p. pr. of stagnare. See Stagnate.]

defs: That stagnates; not flowing; not running in a current or steam; motionless; hence, impure or foul from want of motion; as, a stagnant lake or pond; stagnant blood in the veins.
Not active or brisk; dull; as, business is stagnant.
quotes: That gloomy slumber of the stagnant soul.
For him a stagnant life was not worth living.
